preamble: The diphthong /au/ can undergo a phonetic transformation to /ai/, a shift prominently observed in historical linguistics, particularly as a result of umlaut processes. This change involves the fronting and unrounding of the diphthong's offglide, and often a fronting of the initial vowel component as well.
phoneticEffects: Fronting of the back vowel offglide, unrounding of the rounded vowel offglide, and often fronting of the initial vowel component

examples.note: The irregular pluralization of 'mouse' to 'mice' is a classic example of i-umlaut in Germanic languages, where an earlier /uː/ (or the offglide of /au/) was fronted and unrounded due to a following front vowel, resulting in a shift from a back-rounded diphthong to a front-unrounded one.
